inline_visual.content: One trigger: 40 hours in a workweek. No daily threshold. 1.5x from hour 41.

pullquote_provenance.label: Teamed Legal Operations
pullquote_provenance.mi_query_note: No MI query run. Anonymised per pullquote provenance rule. No fabricated cite line.
pullquote_provenance.content_summary: Tip-credit model and 24-hour final-pay window as the two most common Minnesota payroll errors from out-of-state employers.

editor_notes: Clean first draft against rubric. No em-dashes in body prose. Minimum wage factual uncertainty handled by directing to MN DLI rather than hardcoding a disputed figure.

linter_result.notes: No em-dashes detected in body prose. No forbidden words. No forbidden phrases. Answer-first pattern confirmed for all H2 sections. All CTAs canonical.

audit_score_notes: Points deducted: factual_uncertainty on MN minimum wage 2026 (primary source not directly fetched; two sources disagree). City ordinance exact figures (Minneapolis 6-hr meal break threshold, city wage floor figures) not hardcoded — correct editorial choice but reduces specificity. Hero from different Unsplash photographer than MN sibling (intended — visual variety). All Teamed-difference rules satisfied. Schema full stack. Hero photo present (321KB). Inline visual present (pad-note). All CTAs canonical.

high_concern_flags.detail: Two sources disagree: allvoices.co / workyard.com cite $11.41/hr; other sources cite $11.13/hr. MN DLI primary source (dli.mn.gov) was not directly fetched at build time (access restriction observed). Page intentionally does NOT hardcode a specific figure — directs all readers to dli.mn.gov. This is the correct editorial call for an unresolved primary-source conflict. Verify before promoting to Google indexing.
high_concern_flags.action: Fetch https://www.dli.mn.gov/business/employment-practices/minimum-wage directly and update page with the confirmed 2026 figure. Then remove this flag.

high_concern_flags.detail: Minneapolis Workplace Health and Safety 6-hour meal break threshold cited from ordinance commentary. Primary Minneapolis Code of Ordinances text was not directly verified at build time.
high_concern_flags.action: Verify 6-hour threshold against Minneapolis Code of Ordinances or Minneapolis Labour Standards office before publishing the Minneapolis meal-break claim.

high_concern_flags.detail: United States overview (/hire-in-united-states/) is wave-3. Minnesota state overview (/hire-in-united-states/minnesota/) is wave-4. Both rendered as plain text in the links-rail per link gate rule.

prompt_corrections.prompt_said: Large/small employer no longer distinguished from 1 Aug 2024 amendment (Chapter 116/2023)
prompt_corrections.verified: Correct — confirmed via compliance cache. Chapter 116/2023 effective 1 August 2024 eliminated the split.
prompt_corrections.action: None — page copy reflects this accurately.

prompt_corrections.prompt_said: 48-hr OT threshold (different from federal 40-hr — but 40-hr applies to FLSA-covered employers, which is most)
prompt_corrections.verified: The 48-hr threshold appears in MN Stat. § 177.25 for employers NOT covered by FLSA. For the overwhelming majority of employees hired via EOR, FLSA coverage means the 40-hr threshold governs. Page correctly leads with 40-hr as the operative rule for FLSA-covered employers and notes the non-FLSA edge case.
prompt_corrections.action: No correction needed — page framing is accurate. Confirm the 48-hr non-FLSA threshold before adding to page if needed for edge cases.
